comment: "I found the mechanisms of action of drugs to be the most interesting aspect of this course. I was also particularly interested in learning about drug indications beyond their primary use, such as Topiramate for migraine prevention and amphetamines for ADHD and narcolepsy. Additionally, studying adverse effects and contraindications was valuable because it reinforces safe prescribing and patient-centered care."

"One of the most interesting aspects of the course was learning about the triage system and how it helps prioritize patient care in high-pressure situations. Understanding how to quickly assess and categorize injuries using systems like START gave me a clear picture of how critical thinking and rapid decision-making are applied in real-life emergencies. I also found the team coordination and role assignment during mass casualty incidents particularly fascinating. Additionally, the integration of mental health support for both victims and responders was a powerful reminder of the emotional impact of disasters."

"I found the sections on wound assessment and classification particularly interesting, as they provided a structured approach to understanding different types of wounds and their healing stages. The discussions on wound dressings and their selection based on exudate levels and wound type were also very engaging, as they helped me understand how to choose the right treatment for optimal healing. Additionally, learning about infection control in wound care was insightful, as it emphasized the importance of preventing complications while promoting healing."

"The most interesting aspect of the course for me was the management and treatment of wounds. I found it very informative to learn the different types of wounds, appropriate dressing selection and proper techniques to promote faster healing and prevent infection."
"The part I found most interesting was the types of wound debridements , it introduced me to many wound debridements that I was not familiar with, and it was very enlightening."
